UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile and basic preferences within the SmartExpense application. This entity links to all other user-specific financial data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for authentication and communication.

displayName.description: The user's display name or full name.

currencyPreference.description: The preferred currency for displaying financial values (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Account.title: Account
Account.description: Represents a financial account owned by a user, such as cash, bank, or savings accounts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Account entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this account belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Account)

name.description: The user-friendly name of the account (e.g., 'Cash', 'My Bank Account', 'Savings Fund').

type.description: The type of the account (e.g., 'Cash', 'Bank', 'Credit Card', 'Savings', 'Investment').

initialBalance.description: The balance of the account when it was first created or tracked.

currentBalance.description: The current balance of the account, updated with transactions.

currency.description: The ISO 4217 currency code for the account (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the account was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a classification for transactions (e.g., 'Food', 'Salary', 'Rent'). Categories can be predefined or user-created.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile if this is a custom category, or null if it's a global predefined category. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Category)

name.description: The name of the category (e.g., 'Groceries', 'Utilities', 'Freelance Income').

type.description: The type of category, either 'income' or 'expense'.

icon.description: Optional icon identifier for UI representation of the category.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the category was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the category was last updated.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a single financial event, either an income or an expense, linked to an account and category.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this transaction. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Transaction)

accountId.description: Reference to the Account from which the transaction occurred or to which it was added. (Relationship: Account 1:N Transaction)

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category for this transaction. (Relationship: Category 1:N Transaction)

amount.description: The monetary value of the transaction. Positive for income, negative for expense.

type.description: Indicates if the transaction is an 'income' or 'expense'.

description.description: A brief description or note about the transaction.

date.description: The date and time when the transaction occurred.

recurringTransactionId.description: Optional reference to a RecurringTransaction if this transaction was generated from a recurring template. (Relationship: RecurringTransaction 1:N Transaction)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction record was last updated.

RecurringTransaction.title: RecurringTransaction
RecurringTransaction.description: Defines a template for transactions that occur repeatedly, allowing the app to automatically generate actual Transaction records.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RecurringTransaction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this recurring transaction. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N RecurringTransaction)

accountId.description: Reference to the Account where the recurring transaction will occur. (Relationship: Account 1:N RecurringTransaction)

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category for this recurring transaction. (Relationship: Category 1:N RecurringTransaction)

amount.description: The monetary value of the recurring transaction.

type.description: Indicates if the recurring transaction is an 'income' or 'expense'.

description.description: A brief description or note about the recurring transaction.

startDate.description: The date from which the recurring transaction starts generating actual transactions.

endDate.description: Optional end date for the recurring transaction. If not set, it recurs indefinitely.

nextOccurrenceDate.description: The calculated date of the next upcoming occurrence for this recurring transaction.

lastGeneratedDate.description: The date when a Transaction was last generated from this recurring template.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the recurring transaction record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the recurring transaction record was last updated.

Budget.title: Budget
Budget.description: Represents a user's spending budget for a specific category within a given month.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Budget entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who set this budget. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Budget)

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this budget applies to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Budget)

month.description: The month and year this budget applies to (e.g., '2023-10').

limitAmount.description: The maximum amount budgeted for this category in the specified month.

spentAmount.description: The actual amount spent in this category for the specified month, derived from transactions.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the budget was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the budget was last updated.

SavingsGoal.title: SavingsGoal
SavingsGoal.description: Represents a user-defined financial goal to save a specific amount of money by a target date.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SavingsGoal entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this savings goal. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N SavingsGoal)

name.description: A descriptive name for the savings goal (e.g., 'New Car Fund', 'Vacation to Hawaii').

targetAmount.description: The total amount of money the user aims to save for this goal.

currentAmount.description: The current amount saved towards the goal.

targetDate.description: The desired date by which the savings goal should be achieved.

startDate.description: The date when the savings goal was initiated.

accountId.description: Optional reference to an Account where the savings for this goal are held. (Relationship: Account 1:N SavingsGoal)

status.description: The current status of the goal (e.g., 'active', 'achieved', 'cancelled').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the savings goal was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the savings goal was last updated.

Reminder.title: Reminder
Reminder.description: Represents a user-defined reminder for bills, due dates, or general financial alerts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Reminder entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this reminder. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Reminder)

title.description: A short, descriptive title for the reminder (e.g., 'Rent Due', 'Credit Card Payment').

description.description: An optional detailed description of the reminder.

dueDate.description: The date and time when the reminder is due.

isRecurring.description: Indicates if this is a recurring reminder.

frequency.description: If 'isRecurring' is true, specifies how often the reminder recurs (e.g., 'daily', 'weekly', 'monthly').

nextReminderDate.description: The calculated date and time for the next occurrence of this reminder.

isCompleted.description: Indicates if the reminder has been marked as completed for its current occurrence.

relatedTransactionId.description: Optional reference to a Transaction that this reminder is associated with (e.g., marking a bill as paid). (Relationship: Transaction 1:N Reminder)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the reminder was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the reminder was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID. Includes denormalized 'id' field matching the path wildcard for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores predefined, global expense/income categories accessible to all authenticated users. The 'userId' field will be null or omitted for these global categories. Read-only for authenticated users.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique ID of the global category.

definition.description: Stores user-defined custom expense/income categories. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, matching the path wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this category.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique ID of the custom category.

definition.description: Stores individual financial accounts (e.g., cash, bank, savings) for a user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, matching the path wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this account.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique ID of the financial account.

definition.description: Stores all income and expense transactions for a user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, matching the path wildcard. Also includes 'accountId' and 'categoryId' as foreign keys.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this transaction.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique ID of the transaction.

definition.description: Stores templates for recurring financial events for a user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, matching the path wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this recurring transaction.

params.name: recurringTransactionId
params.description: The unique ID of the recurring transaction template.

definition.description: Stores user-defined monthly budgets per category. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, matching the path wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who set this budget.

params.name: budgetId
params.description: The unique ID of the budget.

definition.description: Stores user's financial savings goals. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, matching the path wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this savings goal.

params.name: savingsGoalId
params.description: The unique ID of the savings goal.

definition.description: Stores user-defined financial reminders and alerts. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, matching the path wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who created this reminder.

params.name: reminderId
params.description: The unique ID of the reminder.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed around user-centric data ownership, ensuring robust security, scalability, and debuggability in alignment with the core design principles. All user-specific data is deeply nested under a `/users/{userId}/` path, making authorization explicit and efficient.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
Each user-owned document (e.g., Account, Transaction, RecurringTransaction, Budget, SavingsGoal, Reminder, and user-defined Categories) explicitly includes a `userId` field that matches the `{userId}` wildcard in its path. This is a critical denormalization strategy. For example, a document at `/users/{userId}/accounts/{accountId}` will have `resource.data.userId == userId`. This design eliminates the need for `get()` calls in Firestore security rules to check parent document ownership. Rules can simply verify `request.auth.uid == userId` (from the path) and `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` (from the document itself). This makes authorization checks atomic, independent of other documents, and highly performant, directly addressing the CRITICAL principle of Authorization Independence.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) and Structural Segregation:**
This structure directly supports Query-as-Permission (QAP) principles by ensuring that the data's location inherently defines its access permissions, allowing secure `list` operations:

1.  **User-Owned Collections:** Collections like `/users/{userId}/accounts`, `/users/{userId}/transactions`, `/users/{userId}/budgets`, etc., naturally segregate data by owner. A query to `/users/{currentUserId}/accounts` will only ever return accounts belonging to `currentUserId`. The security rule `allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;` ensures that users can only list their own data. This means the client-side query itself does not need to apply filters for `userId`; the structure and rules enforce it. This prevents scenarios where rules might attempt to 'filter out' unauthorized documents from a broader result set (which Firestore rules cannot do), thereby strictly adhering to QAPs.

2.  **Segregated Categories:** The `Category` entity is handled with structural segregation to accommodate both global, predefined categories and user-specific custom categories. Global categories reside in a top-level collection `/categories/{categoryId}`, which will be read-only for all authenticated users. User-defined categories are nested under `/users/{userId}/categories/{categoryId}`, providing read/write access only to the owning user. This separation ensures that a `list` query on `/categories` will only return global items, and a `list` on `/users/{userId}/categories` will only return the user's custom items, without mixing security postures in a single collection. This is a prime example of structural segregation enabling clear, simple, and QAP-compliant `list` rules.

In summary, the design prioritizes path-based ownership, consistent denormalization of the `userId` in all subcollection documents, and strategic segregation of data with different access requirements. This approach ensures that security rules are simple, auditable, and inherently prevent unauthorized access while supporting efficient client-side querying without relying on server-side filtering by rules.
